sections.text: This Act may be cited as the Small Claims Courts Act.

(1) In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires— “ active politics ” has the meaning assigned to the words in the Benefits of Former Presidents Act; [Cap. 15 ] [definition of “active politics” inserted by section 2(b) of Act 15 of 2025 ] “ agent ” means a person authorised by a body corporate to appear before a small claims court on behalf of that body corporate but does not include a legal practitioner ; [definition of “agent” inserted by section 2(b) of Act 15 of 2025 ] " arbitrator " [definition of "arbitrator" deleted by section 2(a) of Act 14 of 2008 ] " award " [definition of "award" deleted by section 2(a) of Act 14 of 2008 ] " Commission "  means the Judicial Service Commission established under the Constitution; [Cap. 1] [definition of "Commission" substituted by section 2(a) of Act 15 of 2025 ] " commissioner " means a person appointed by the Commission under section six; [definition of "commissioner" inserted by section 2(b) of Act 14 of 2008 ] “ Emoluments Commission ” means the Emoluments Commission established under the Constitution; [Cap. 1] [definition of  “Emoluments Commission” inserted by section 2(b) of Act 15 of 2025 ] " judgment " means, the decision made by the commissioner under section twenty in favour of either party to a claim; and [definition of "judgment" inserted by section 2(b) of Act 14 of 2008 ] “ judicial officer ” has the meaning assigned to the words in the Constitution; [Cap. 1] [definition of  “judicial officer” inserted by section 2(b) of Act 15 of 2025 ] “ legally disqualified ” means the absence of legal capacity as provided under section 4 of the Mental Health Act; [Cap.  305] [definition of  “legally disqualified” inserted by section 2(b) of Act 15 of 2025 ] " practitioner " shall have the meaning assigned to it by section two of the Legal Practitioners Act; and "legal practitioner" shall be construed accordingly; “ research advocate ” means a person appointed as a research advocate in a superior court by the Commission ; [definition of “research advocate” inserted by section 2(b) of Act 15 of 2025 ] " small claim "  means a liquidated claim or counter claim that does not exceed the sum that the Chief Justice may, by statutory instrument, prescribe; and [definition of "small claim" inserted by section 2(b) of Act 14 of 2008 and substituted by section 2(a) of Act 15 of 2025 ] " small claims court officer " means an officer appointed under section ten; " small claims court " means a court established under section three; " subordinate court " means a subordinate court constituted under the Subordinate Courts Act. [Cap. 30; Cap. 28]
In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires— “ active politics ” has the meaning assigned to the words in the Benefits of Former Presidents Act; [Cap. 15 ] [definition of “active politics” inserted by section 2(b) of Act 15 of 2025 ] “ agent ” means a person authorised by a body corporate to appear before a small claims court on behalf of that body corporate but does not include a legal practitioner ; [definition of “agent” inserted by section 2(b) of Act 15 of 2025 ] " arbitrator " [definition of "arbitrator" deleted by section 2(a) of Act 14 of 2008 ] " award " [definition of "award" deleted by section 2(a) of Act 14 of 2008 ] " Commission "  means the Judicial Service Commission established under the Constitution; [Cap. 1] [definition of "Commission" substituted by section 2(a) of Act 15 of 2025 ] " commissioner " means a person appointed by the Commission under section six; [definition of "commissioner" inserted by section 2(b) of Act 14 of 2008 ] “ Emoluments Commission ” means the Emoluments Commission established under the Constitution; [Cap. 1] [definition of  “Emoluments Commission” inserted by section 2(b) of Act 15 of 2025 ] " judgment " means, the decision made by the commissioner under section twenty in favour of either party to a claim; and [definition of "judgment" inserted by section 2(b) of Act 14 of 2008 ] “ judicial officer ” has the meaning assigned to the words in the Constitution; [Cap. 1] [definition of  “judicial officer” inserted by section 2(b) of Act 15 of 2025 ] “ legally disqualified ” means the absence of legal capacity as provided under section 4 of the Mental Health Act; [Cap.  305] [definition of  “legally disqualified” inserted by section 2(b) of Act 15 of 2025 ] " practitioner " shall have the meaning assigned to it by section two of the Legal Practitioners Act; and "legal practitioner" shall be construed accordingly; “ research advocate ” means a person appointed as a research advocate in a superior court by the Commission ; [definition of “research advocate” inserted by section 2(b) of Act 15 of 2025 ] " small claim "  means a liquidated claim or counter claim that does not exceed the sum that the Chief Justice may, by statutory instrument, prescribe; and [definition of "small claim" inserted by section 2(b) of Act 14 of 2008 and substituted by section 2(a) of Act 15 of 2025 ] " small clai
There is hereby established small claims courts which shall be situated in such areas as the Chief Justice may consider necessary, having regard to the needs of a particular area.
A commissioner , sitting alone, shall constitute a small claims court . [section 4 amended by section 3 of Act 14 of 2008 ]
Any provision in an agreement purporting to exclude the jurisdiction of a small claims court shall be of no  effect. [section 5A inserted by section 5 of Act 14 of 2008 ]

(1) The Commission shall, in consultation with the Chief Justice, appoint such number of commissioners for a small claims court.
The Commission shall, in consultation with the Chief Justice, appoint such number of commissioners for a small claims court.
(2) A commissioner appointed under subsection (1) shall be appointed on a part-time basis.
A commissioner appointed under subsection (1) shall be appointed on a part-time basis.
(3) The Chief Justice may, by Gazette Notice, designate a judicial officer or research advocate to— (a) hear and determine an action over a small claim; and (b) carry out the duties in the small claims court that the Chief Justice may determine.
The Chief Justice may, by Gazette Notice, designate a judicial officer or research advocate to—
(a) hear and determine an action over a small claim; and
hear and determine an action over a small claim; and
(b) carry out the duties in the small claims court that the Chief Justice may determine.
carry out the duties in the small claims court that the Chief Justice may determine.
(4) A judicial officer or research advocate designated under subsection (3) shall be appointed on a part-time basis.
A judicial officer or research advocate designated under subsection (3) shall be appointed on a part-time basis.

(1) In an action in which the sum claimed does not exceed the jurisdiction of the court and is the balance of an account, the court may enquire into and hear evidence upon the whole account, even though that account relates to items and transactions exceeding the jurisdiction of the court.
In an action in which the sum claimed does not exceed the jurisdiction of the court and is the balance of an account, the court may enquire into and hear evidence upon the whole account, even though that account relates to items and transactions exceeding the jurisdiction of the court.
(2) Where the amount claimed or other relief sought does not exceed the jurisdiction of the court, the court shall not pe deprived of that jurisdiction merely because it is necessary for the court, in order to arrive at a decision, to give a finding upon a matter beyond its jurisdiction.
Where the amount claimed or other relief sought does not exceed the jurisdiction of the court, the court shall not pe deprived of that jurisdiction merely because it is necessary for the court, in order to arrive at a decision, to give a finding upon a matter beyond its jurisdiction.
(3) The plaintiff may only claim for the relief of specific performance if such claim is accompanied by an alternative claim for payment of quantified damages that the plaintiff has suffered as a result of the defendant's failure to comply with the plaintiffs obligations.
The plaintiff may only claim for the relief of specific performance if such claim is accompanied by an alternative claim for payment of quantified damages that the plaintiff has suffered as a result of the defendant's failure to comply with the plaintiffs obligations.
A person shall not be appointed as a commissioner unless the person is a legal practitioner of not less than five years standing. [section 7 substituted by section 8 of Act 14 of 2008 ]

A person shall not sit as a commissioner in any matter in which that person is a party, or in which that person has a direct or indirect pecuniary or personal interest. [section 9 substituted by section 10 of Act 14 of 2008 ]

(1) The Commission may appoint such number of officers as the Commission may consider necessary for the administration of this Act. [subsection (1) substituted by section 11 of Act 14 of 2008 ]
The Commission may appoint such number of officers as the Commission may consider necessary for the administration of this Act. [subsection (1) substituted by section 11 of Act 14 of 2008 ]
(2) The small claims courts officers shall exercise such powers and perform such duties as are conferred or imposed upon them by or under the provisions of this Act.
The small claims courts officers shall exercise such powers and perform such duties as are conferred or imposed upon them by or under the provisions of this Act.
A small claims court shall sit at such times and places as may be necessary for the convenient and speedy despatch of business.

(1) Proceedings in a small claims court shall be held in open court.
Proceedings in a small claims court shall be held in open court.
(2) The proceedings shall be simple and informal.
The proceedings shall be simple and informal.

(1) A person may institute an action in the small claims court or become a party to an action before the small claims court, as a defendant.
A person may institute an action in the small claims court or become a party to an action before the small claims court, as a defendant.

(1) No legal practitioner , other than a practitioner who is a party acting solely on his own behalf, may appear or act before a small claims court on behalf of any party to the proceedings.
No legal practitioner , other than a practitioner who is a party acting solely on his own behalf, may appear or act before a small claims court on behalf of any party to the proceedings.
(2) Subject to subsection (1) , a small claims court may permit any person who is the spouse, guardian or other member of the household of a party in any proceedings before the court to appear or act for that party, if he satisfies the court that he has the authority of the party to so appear or act.
Subject to subsection (1) , a small claims court may permit any person who is the spouse, guardian or other member of the household of a party in any proceedings before the court to appear or act for that party, if he satisfies the court that he has the authority of the party to so appear or act.
(3) Subject to subsection (1) , a body corporate or an unincorparated body which is a party to the proceeding before a small claims court may be represented by an— (a) officer or employee of that body corporate or unincorporated body; or (b) agent who is authorised by that body corporate or unincorporated body to appear on its behalf. [subsection (3) substituted by section 7 of Act 15 of 2025 ]
Subject to subsection (1) , a body corporate or an unincorparated body which is a party to the proceeding before a small claims court may be represented by an—
(a) officer or employee of that body corporate or unincorporated body; or
officer or employee of that body corporate or unincorporated body; or
(b) agent who is authorised by that body corporate or unincorporated body to appear on its behalf.
agent who is authorised by that body corporate or unincorporated body to appear on its behalf.
A small claims court shall, when adjudicating, aim at the reconciliation of the parties and it shall be the primary function of the court to do substantial justice between the parties.

(1) The business of a small claims court shall be conducted in English or in such other language as, in the opinion of the court, is convenient for the court and all the parties before it.
The business of a small claims court shall be conducted in English or in such other language as, in the opinion of the court, is convenient for the court and all the parties before it.
(2) Where a party to the proceedings is not conversant with the language being used in the court, the party shall be allowed, or provided with, an interpreter.
Where a party to the proceedings is not conversant with the language being used in the court, the party shall be allowed, or provided with, an interpreter.
(3) Whenever any language other than English is used in any proceedings, the court shall cause to be made an English translation of the proceedings before it, but shall not maintain a case record of the proceedings.
Whenever any language other than English is used in any proceedings, the court shall cause to be made an English translation of the proceedings before it, but shall not maintain a case record of the proceedings.

(1) The court shall, not be bound by rules of evidence, but shall apply the law and equity.
The court shall, not be bound by rules of evidence, but shall apply the law and equity.
(2) Evidence in a small claims courts shall be given on oath or affirmation.
Evidence in a small claims courts shall be given on oath or affirmation.

(1) Prior to the commencement of an action in the Court, the plaintiff shall cause to be issued and served upon the defendant a letter of demand requiring the defendant, within fourteen days of the receipt of such letter, to admit or deny the claim.
Prior to the commencement of an action in the Court, the plaintiff shall cause to be issued and served upon the defendant a letter of demand requiring the defendant, within fourteen days of the receipt of such letter, to admit or deny the claim.
(2) A defendant shall, within fourteen days of receiving a letter of demand, reply to the letter, and shall indicate whether the defendant admits or denies the claim.
A defendant shall, within fourteen days of receiving a letter of demand, reply to the letter, and shall indicate whether the defendant admits or denies the claim.
(3) The Clerk of Court shall— (a) upon receiving a reply from a defendant, or in default thereof upon receiving proof of service of a letter of demand from a plaintiff; and (b) upon the expiration of fourteen days from the date of service; cause a Notice of Claim to be issued to the defendant.
The Clerk of Court shall—
(a) upon receiving a reply from a defendant, or in default thereof upon receiving proof of service of a letter of demand from a plaintiff; and
upon receiving a reply from a defendant, or in default thereof upon receiving proof of service of a letter of demand from a plaintiff; and
(b) upon the expiration of fourteen days from the date of service;
upon the expiration of fourteen days from the date of service;
(4) A Notice of Claim issued under subsection (3) shall be served upon a defendant in accordance with rules 3 and 4 of the rules of court.
A Notice of Claim issued under subsection (3) shall be served upon a defendant in accordance with rules 3 and 4 of the rules of court.

A claim exceeding the jurisdiction of a small claims court shall not be split with the object of recovering it in more than one action if the parties to those actions and the point at issue in those actions would be the same. [section 16B added by section 13 of Act 14 of 2008 ]

(1) A plaintiff may at any time before or during the hearing of the plaintiff's action, withdraw the plaintiff's claim with the leave of  small claims  court, and on such conditions as the  small claims  court may determine. [subsection (1) amended by section 8 of Act 15 of 2025 ]
A plaintiff may at any time before or during the hearing of the plaintiff's action, withdraw the plaintiff's claim with the leave of  small claims  court, and on such conditions as the  small claims  court may determine. [subsection (1) amended by section 8 of Act 15 of 2025 ]
(2) A plaintiff may, where proceedings are withdrawn under subsection (1) , institute a fresh action with the leave of court.
A plaintiff may, where proceedings are withdrawn under subsection (1) , institute a fresh action with the leave of court.

A party may, in order to bring a claim or counter claim within the jurisdiction of a small claims court , abandon such part of the claim or counter claim as may be necessary. [section 16D added by section 13 of Act 14 of 2008 ]

A small claims court shall hear the facts of the case and receive any documents relating to the claim before it and do all such things as are authorised by this Act or, as are necessary for the exercise of its functions under this Act.

(1) A small claims court may summon any person who is a defendant or witness in any matter before it.
A small claims court may summon any person who is a defendant or witness in any matter before it.
(2) The costs of serving or attempting to serve any summons issued by a small claims court relating to any matter shall be borne by the party at whose instance the summons was issued.
The costs of serving or attempting to serve any summons issued by a small claims court relating to any matter shall be borne by the party at whose instance the summons was issued.
(3) Where a defendant is summoned under subsection (1) , and has reasonable notice of the time and place at which he is required to attend, but fails to attend without any reasonable cause, the court may proceed to hear the matter and may enter judgment in default of appearance and defence if it is satisfied that the summons was properly served on him. [subsection (3) amended by section 14 of Act 14 of 2008 ]
Where a defendant is summoned under subsection (1) , and has reasonable notice of the time and place at which he is required to attend, but fails to attend without any reasonable cause, the court may proceed to hear the matter and may enter judgment in default of appearance and defence if it is satisfied that the summons was properly served on him. [subsection (3) amended by section 14 of Act 14 of 2008 ]

(1) A counter-claim by a defendant may be made in a small claim brought against that defendant by the plaintiff.
A counter-claim by a defendant may be made in a small claim brought against that defendant by the plaintiff.
(2) Where a counter-claim is made by a defendant under subsection (1) , the counter-claim shall not exceed the jurisdictional limit of the small claims court.
Where a counter-claim is made by a defendant under subsection (1) , the counter-claim shall not exceed the jurisdictional limit of the small claims court.

(1) The court may, at any time before judgment , grant leave to amend any notice of claim or other document in connection with a case on application by a party: Provided that an amendment shall not be made where a party other than the party applying for the amendment may be prejudiced by the amendment.
The court may, at any time before judgment , grant leave to amend any notice of claim or other document in connection with a case on application by a party: Provided that an amendment shall not be made where a party other than the party applying for the amendment may be prejudiced by the amendment.
(2) An amendment may be made upon such conditions as the court may consider appropriate.
An amendment may be made upon such conditions as the court may consider appropriate.

A Court may, for good cause shown, on its own motion, and in the absence of any party—
(a) review or vary any judgment made by it;
review or vary any judgment made by it;
(b) review or vary any judgment obtained by fraud or mistake; and
review or vary any judgment obtained by fraud or mistake; and
(c) correct any latent errors in any judgment .
correct any latent errors in any judgment .

The provisions of the High Court Act, Subordinate Court Act and Debtors Act shall apply, with the necessary modifications, to the enforcement of a judgement made under this Act. [Cap. 27; Cap. 28; Cap. 77 ] [section 21 substituted by section 19 of Act 14 of 2008 and by section 10 of Act 15 of 2025 ]
The awards of a small claims court shall be final, but appeal to the High Court shall be allowed on points of law only.

(1) A party may, where a court has adjudicated upon a matter, bring the proceedings on review before a panel of three commissioners within thirty days of the judgement, on any of the following grounds: (a) absence of jurisdiction of the court; (b) that the party was not given an opportunity to make representation to the court; (c) errors on the record; and [paragraph (c) inserted by section 11(a)(i) of Act 15 of 2025 ] (d) corruption, fraud, malice or interest in the cause, on the part of the court; and [paragraph (d) , previously paragraph (c) , renumbered by section 11(a)(ii) of Act 15 of 2025 ] (e) gross irregularity with regard to the proceedings: [paragraph (e) , previously paragraph (d) , renumbered by section 11(a)(ii) of Act 15 of 2025 ] Provided that an application for review by a party shall not operate as a stay of execution.
A party may, where a court has adjudicated upon a matter, bring the proceedings on review before a panel of three commissioners within thirty days of the judgement, on any of the following grounds:
(a) absence of jurisdiction of the court;
absence of jurisdiction of the court;
(b) that the party was not given an opportunity to make representation to the court;
that the party was not given an opportunity to make representation to the court;
(c) errors on the record; and [paragraph (c) inserted by section 11(a)(i) of Act 15 of 2025 ]
errors on the record; and [paragraph (c) inserted by section 11(a)(i) of Act 15 of 2025 ]
(d) corruption, fraud, malice or interest in the cause, on the part of the court; and [paragraph (d) , previously paragraph (c) , renumbered by section 11(a)(ii) of Act 15 of 2025 ]
corruption, fraud, malice or interest in the cause, on the part of the court; and [paragraph (d) , previously paragraph (c) , renumbered by section 11(a)(ii) of Act 15 of 2025 ]
(e) gross irregularity with regard to the proceedings: [paragraph (e) , previously paragraph (d) , renumbered by section 11(a)(ii) of Act 15 of 2025 ]
gross irregularity with regard to the proceedings: [paragraph (e) , previously paragraph (d) , renumbered by section 11(a)(ii) of Act 15 of 2025 ]
(2) Subsection (1) shall not prejudice the right of an applicant to apply to the panel of commissioners for a stay of execution.
Subsection (1) shall not prejudice the right of an applicant to apply to the panel of commissioners for a stay of execution.
(3) Where a matter is brought before a panel of commisioners on review in accordance with subsection (1) , the commissioner that adjudicated on the matter shall not sit on the panel of commissioners on review. [subsection (3) added by section 11(b) of Act 15 of 2025 ]
Where a matter is brought before a panel of commisioners on review in accordance with subsection (1) , the commissioner that adjudicated on the matter shall not sit on the panel of commissioners on review. [subsection (3) added by section 11(b) of Act 15 of 2025 ]

(1) A panel of commissioners may, on review of a judgement, confirm or set aside a judgement, by majority, on satisfying themselves that any of the grounds specified under section 22A are proved. [subsection (1) substituted by section 12(a) of Act 15 of 2025 ]
A panel of commissioners may, on review of a judgement, confirm or set aside a judgement, by majority, on satisfying themselves that any of the grounds specified under section 22A are proved. [subsection (1) substituted by section 12(a) of Act 15 of 2025 ]
(2) A panel of commissioners shall, where it sets aside a judgment under subsection (1) , hear the matter de novo .
A panel of commissioners shall, where it sets aside a judgment under subsection (1) , hear the matter de novo .
(3) A panel of commissioners shall, when reviewing a judgment on the ground of errors on the record, only determine the matter in respect to the errors on the record and shall not hear the matter in full. [subsection (3) added by section 12(b) of Act 15 of 2025 ]
A panel of commissioners shall, when reviewing a judgment on the ground of errors on the record, only determine the matter in respect to the errors on the record and shall not hear the matter in full. [subsection (3) added by section 12(b) of Act 15 of 2025 ]
The court shall not order costs in any matter before it.

The provisions of the High Court Act, Subordinate Court Act and Debtors Act shall apply, with the necessary modifications, to a writ of execution made under this Act. [Cap. 27; Cap. 28; Cap. 77] [section 24 substituted by section 21 of Act 14 of 2008 and by  section 13 of Act 15 of 2025

Any person who obstructs, any officer of a small claims court or other person executing a writ of execution or an order issued or made in the exercise of the powers conferred by this Act, shall be guilty of an offence and shall be liable upon conviction to a fine not exceeding  twenty-five thousand penalty units or to a term of imprisonment not exceeding six months, or to both. [As amended by Act No. 13 of 1994 ] [section 26 amended by section 24 of Act 14 of 2008 and by  section 15 of Act 15 of 2025 ]

Any person who, without lawful authority, purports to exercise judicial functions as a commissioner or, falsely holds himself out to be a commissioner , shall be guilty of an offence and shall be liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding fifty thousand penalty units or to a term of imprisonment not exceeding twelve months, or to both. [As amended by Act No. 13 of 1994 ] [section 27 amended by section 24 of Act 14 of 2008 and by section 16 of Act 15 of 2025 ]

The funds of the small claims court consist of monies that may—
(a) be appropriated by Parliament;
be appropriated by Parliament;
(b) be paid by way of fees to a small claims court in respect of proceedings before it; and
be paid by way of fees to a small claims court in respect of proceedings before it; and
(c) vest in, or accrue to, the small claims court.
vest in, or accrue to, the small claims court.

(1) The administrative expenses of a small claims court shall be a charge on the general revenues of the Republic.
The administrative expenses of a small claims court shall be a charge on the general revenues of the Republic.
(2) A party shall pay a prescribed fee when lodging a document under this Act. [subsection (2) amended by section 25 of Act 14 of 2008 and substituted by section 18 of Act 15 of 2025 ]
A party shall pay a prescribed fee when lodging a document under this Act. [subsection (2) amended by section 25 of Act 14 of 2008 and substituted by section 18 of Act 15 of 2025 ]

No action or other proceedings shall lie or be instituted against a commissioner or any other officer of a small claims court for, or in respect of, any act or thing done or omitted to be done in good faith in the exercise or purported exercise of the functions of the officer under this Act. [section 30 substituted by section 26 of Act 14 of 2008 ]

(1) The conduct of commissioners shall be governed by the Judicial Code of Conduct Act, 1999.
The conduct of commissioners shall be governed by the Judicial Code of Conduct Act, 1999.
(2) Notwithstanding the provisions of subsection (1) , sections sixteen, seventeen and eighteen of the Judicial Code of Conduct Act, 1999, shall not apply to the conduct of commissioners.
Notwithstanding the provisions of subsection (1) , sections sixteen, seventeen and eighteen of the Judicial Code of Conduct Act, 1999, shall not apply to the conduct of commissioners.
